description: Original short bilingual dialogues aligned with the main concepts in Language Transfer Spanish lessons 1–68.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: I want to prepare a presentation.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: I don't want to cancel the meeting.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: I want to confirm the organization.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: I want to find out about the situation.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: I want to explain something to you.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: I don't want to oblige you.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Do you want to experience it?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I want to experience it.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are you going to the hospital now?

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: What are you trying to do?

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are you trying to publish it today?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I'm trying to publish it.

dialogues.title: Voy a and verbs of movement

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: I'm going to stop by to visit you.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Perfect. I want to show you something.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are you going to continue with the project?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I'm going to try to continue it.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: I'm also going to examine it.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Do you want to come and visit me?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I must do it today.

lines.speaker: A
lines.spanish: Quiero saber si quiere venir a la casa.
lines.english: I want to know if he wants to come to the house.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: He doesn't want to come now.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Why doesn't he want to come?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: He doesn't want to explain it.

lines.speaker: A
lines.spanish: Quiero saber por qué no quiere explicarlo.
lines.english: I want to know why he doesn't want to explain it.

dialogues.title: Verb patterns, the future and tener

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: I'm going to organize a celebration.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are you going to have time?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I'm going to have time.

dialogues.title: Verb groups, -ar endings and object placement

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: No, I'm not speaking with Pablo.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Do you want to see him?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I want to see him.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are you going to invite him?

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are they selling it to us?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: No, they aren't selling it to us.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Why aren't they selling it to us?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: I don't know. I want to know.

dialogues.title: Sé, pronouns, tengo que and poner

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Do you know what she wants?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: I don't know what she wants.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Entonces pongo la comida en la mesa.
lines.english: Then I'll put the food on the table.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: I think we're going to lose.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: No. I fall asleep very late.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: When do you start the work?

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Will you show me a sample?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I'll show it to you tomorrow.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: What are we having to drink?

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: What do we prefer to do?

dialogues.title: Working backward to find verbs and genders

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Voy a una cena esta noche.
lines.english: I'm going to a dinner tonight.

dialogues.title: Using the present tense for future events
focus: present tense with future time expressions

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Nos vemos la semana que viene.
lines.english: We'll see each other next week.

dialogues.title: Using the present perfect for past events

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I have spoken with her.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: No, todavía no lo han terminado.
lines.english: No, they still haven't finished it.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: No, ellos no me han visto.

dialogues.title: Masculine words ending in -ma and sentir

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, pero no he entendido el problema.
lines.english: Yes, but I haven't understood the problem.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I like it a lot.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Will you give me the report tomorrow?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I'll give it to you tomorrow.

lines.speaker: A
lines.spanish: ¿Ustedes le dan una copia a Ana?
lines.english: Are you all giving Ana a copy?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, we'll give it to her tomorrow.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Will you give us a copy too?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, también se la damos a ustedes.
lines.english: Yes, we'll give one to you all too.

dialogues.title: Cocinar, quedar and telling the time

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, me quedo hasta las nueve.

lines.speaker: A
lines.spanish: Tengo que quedarme aquí esta noche.
lines.english: I have to stay here tonight.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Why do you have to stay?

lines.speaker: A
lines.spanish: Tengo que llamar a un cliente.
lines.english: I have to call a client.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Do you want to buy the digital camera?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: No, I want the small camera.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Do you prefer the big houses?

dialogues.title: Adjective agreement and words ending in -ive

lines.speaker: A
lines.spanish: La profesora nueva es muy creativa.
lines.english: The new teacher is very creative.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, y los estudiantes son muy activos.
lines.english: Yes, and the students are very active.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, they are positive and intuitive.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are they going to be here later?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, van a estar aquí toda la tarde.
lines.english: Yes, they're going to be here all afternoon.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: They are intelligent and very kind.

dialogues.title: Changing meaning with ser and estar

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, estoy libre después de las cuatro.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Está feliz; normalmente es una persona feliz.
lines.english: He's happy; normally he's a happy person.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are you preparing it tomorrow too?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: No, Ana is preparing it tomorrow.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are you being sincere with me?

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Will you go to Madrid tomorrow?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I'll go in the morning.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I'll stay for two nights.

dialogues.title: Lo and le
focus: speaking to and giving to someone

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Do you speak to him every day?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, le hablo por la mañana.
lines.english: Yes, I speak to him in the morning.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are you giving him the report today?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, le doy el informe esta tarde.
lines.english: Yes, I'll give him the report this afternoon.

dialogues.title: When le and les become se

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are you giving Ana the book?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I'm giving it to her.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are you giving them the keys?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I'm giving them to them.

dialogues.title: Prepositions: with, for, from and to

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Entonces voy a hablar con ella.
lines.english: Then I'm going to speak with her.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: I think about you a lot.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Do you want to come with me tomorrow?

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Does it depend on whether she comes?

dialogues.title: Will, would and reflexive meaning
focus: se for changes affecting the subject

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Would you dance with me tonight?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I would dance with you.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Would you also go to the party?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: No, no iría porque estoy cansado.
lines.english: No, I wouldn't go because I'm tired.

dialogues.title: To like, would like and could

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I like it a lot.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Would you like to eat here tomorrow?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, he should know about it.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Would you have gone with us?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I would have gone with you.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: We would have liked to see you.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: A mí también me habría gustado.
lines.english: I would have liked that too.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Do you want this car or that car?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Quiero ese; este es demasiado grande.
lines.english: I want that one; this one is too big.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, it interests me a lot.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: What do you think of the idea?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: No, it doesn't seem convenient to her.

dialogues.title: The line in the past with -ar verbs

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did you use to work here?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, trabajaba aquí todos los días.
lines.english: Yes, I used to work here every day.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did you wait for Pablo in the morning?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, lo esperaba a las ocho.
lines.english: Yes, I used to wait for him at eight.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Were they trying to find the problem?

dialogues.title: The line in the past with reflexive verbs

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did you use to stay here during the summer?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, me quedaba con mis amigos.
lines.english: Yes, I used to stay with my friends.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, se quemaba cuando hablábamos por teléfono.
lines.english: Yes, it burned when we spoke on the phone.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, se rompían todo el tiempo.
lines.english: Yes, they broke all the time.

dialogues.title: The line in the past with -er and -ir verbs

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did you use to eat here when you were a student?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, comía aquí todos los días.
lines.english: Yes, I used to eat here every day.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, se vendía por un precio muy bajo.
lines.english: Yes, it was for sale at a very low price.

dialogues.title: Imperfect forms of llamar, tener, ver, ser and ir

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did you have classes every day?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, tenía clases por la mañana.
lines.english: Yes, I had classes in the morning.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did you use to go by bus?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, e iba viendo la ciudad por la ventana.
lines.english: Yes, and I used to watch the city through the window.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Thank you. Did you buy it because of me?

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Yes, I bought it because of you.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Are you staying for many days?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Entonces esta habitación es para ti.
lines.english: Then this room is for you.

dialogues.title: Para or por, part two, and false friends

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Would you exchange this book for that one?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I would exchange this one for that one.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Currently I work at a school.

dialogues.title: Para as in order to and more gerunds

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Estaba tomando un curso de español.
lines.english: I was taking a Spanish course.

dialogues.title: Could, had and would in the past

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: No podía porque no tenía tiempo.
lines.english: I couldn't because I didn't have time.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Would you have gone with more time?

dialogues.title: Should have, could have and irregular participles

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Lo sé. Podría haber llamado ayer.
lines.english: I know. I could have called yesterday.

lines.speaker: A
lines.spanish: ¿Le has dicho la verdad a Ana?
lines.english: Have you told Ana the truth?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I have told it to her.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Trabajo con la chica que conociste ayer.
lines.english: I work with the girl you met yesterday.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Who are you preparing the report for?

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Which of these reports does she want?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: I don't know which one she wants.

lines.speaker: A
lines.spanish: Lo bueno es que todos viven cerca.
lines.english: The good thing is that everyone lives nearby.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Lo malo es que nunca llegan a tiempo.
lines.english: The bad thing is that they never arrive on time.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Shall we talk about the party situation?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, pero no quiero hablarlo así.
lines.english: Yes, but I don't want to discuss it like this.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I'll organize it this afternoon.

dialogues.title: The dot in the past with -ar verbs

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did Ana speak with you yesterday?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, habló conmigo por la tarde.
lines.english: Yes, she spoke with me in the afternoon.

dialogues.title: The dot in the past with -er and -ir verbs

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, she went out at eight.

dialogues.title: The dot in the past with nosotros

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did we speak with Ana yesterday?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, we spoke for an hour.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, we ate at her house.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did we get lost on the way back?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: No, esta vez no nos perdimos.
lines.english: No, this time we didn't get lost.

dialogues.title: When the line and the dot meet

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: What were you doing when Ana called?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: I was cooking when the phone rang.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: It seemed good to me at first.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Cuando la vi, no me gustó.
lines.english: When I saw it, I didn't like it.

dialogues.title: Gave and why le becomes se

lines.speaker: A
lines.spanish: ¿Quién le dio el libro a Ana?

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: And who gave her the keys?

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did he give us a copy too?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, he gave it to us yesterday.

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: No, I don't remember it well.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, a ella le gustó mucho.
lines.english: Yes, she liked it a lot.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: A él no le gustó la música.

dialogues.title: Past events with tú and ellos

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did you eat before going out?

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did you go out last night?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Ellos comieron y salieron hace dos horas.
lines.english: They ate and went out two hours ago.

dialogues.title: Nothing else, another time and possessives

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: No, I didn't eat anything else.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, lo vi otra vez en tu casa.
lines.english: Yes, I saw him again at your house.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Did everyone arrive at the same time?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, todos llegaron a mi casa a la vez.
lines.english: Yes, everyone arrived at my house at the same time.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Are we going to José's house?

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: No, vamos a la fiesta de María.
lines.english: No, we're going to María's party.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: No, es el coche de Ana.

dialogues.title: Written Spanish and accent marks

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: I said ‘yes’, with an accent mark.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Do you know where your book is?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: Yes, I know where it is.

dialogues.notes: This dialogue deliberately contrasts words whose accent marks change their meaning.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: Do you want this book or that one?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: I want that one; I already have this one.

lines.speaker: A
lines.english: How many times have you read it?

lines.speaker: B
lines.english: I don't know how many times.

lines.speaker: B
lines.spanish: Sí, me gustó tanto que lo leí otra vez.
lines.english: Yes, I liked it so much that I read it again.
